description_long: This is a large, rustic cabin set on a polygonal grassy plot with a dark base, featuring a sprawling, multi-gabled roof covered in textured brown shingles. The structure is primarily constructed from wood, with visible log or timber framing and deep overhanging eaves. Multiple sections of the building are interconnected, creating a complex footprint with several wings and alcoves. Large windows and glass doors are present, especially prominent on one facade, allowing for ample natural light and scenic views. A covered porch or veranda runs along at least one side, supported by wooden posts and beams, enhancing the cabin's welcoming appearance. The surrounding terrain is grassy, and a paved driveway or walkway leads up to the main entrance, suggesting accessibility for vehicles and pedestrians.
description: A sprawling, rustic wooden cabin with a multi-gabled shingle roof, large windows, and a covered porch, set on a grassy plot with a paved driveway.
description_view_0: This view highlights the cabin's long side with a prominent covered porch, visible timber supports, and the main driveway leading up to the entrance.
description_view_1: From this angle, the cabin's rear facade is visible, showcasing large glass doors and windows, and the complex, multi-gabled roof structure.
description_view_2: This side view emphasizes the interconnected wings of the cabin, the extended rooflines, and additional window groupings along the facade.
description_view_3: The front approach is visible here, with the driveway, main entrance, and porch area clearly shown, as well as the overall depth of the building.
attribute_roof_type: multi-gabled, intersecting gable roof with deep overhangs and textured shingle covering
attribute_window_arrangement: Multiple large and medium-sized rectangular windows, asymmetrically distributed, with some grouped in clusters and others as single units, especially prominent on the rear and side facades
attribute_door_placement: Main entrance door located on the front facade adjacent to the driveway and porch; additional glass doors visible on the rear facade
